full_prompt: Design a 1-meter cylindrical airframe tube section of a shoulder-launched anti-UAV missile (MANPADS-class UAS interceptor) benchmark, referenced to modern graduate missile-design standards. Total missile launch mass <= 15 kg (incl. warhead + motor + seeker + airframe), tube outer diameter 100 mm, engagement slant range 4000 m, peak acceleration at motor burnout 40 g axial, maneuver capability 20 g lateral at Mach 2.0 terminal engagement, IR seeker dome forward of the frame, launcher tube inner diameter 105 mm. Material: filament-wound carbon/epoxy composite cylinder [+/-45/0/90]s layup, quasi-isotropic effective E1=E2=55 GPa, G12=20 GPa, Poisson 0.30, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile strength 800 MPa, compressive 600 MPa, through-thickness thermal conductivity 0.5 W/mK. Tube wall nominal 3 mm, reinforced at four hardpoints where fin-mount lugs interface. Tube must survive aerodynamic skin heating of 250 C at Mach 2.0 over a 6-second powered fly-out. Load cases: (LC1) launch eject + burnout: 40 g axial on 15 kg mass = 5886 N axial distributed through tube; (LC2) terminal maneuver: 20 g lateral on 10 kg post-burn mass = 1962 N lateral applied at the CG 0.6 m aft of nose; (LC3) aerodynamic pressure: external q = 250 kPa at Mach 2.0 sea level terminal phase, equivalent to 79 kN distributed across 1 m tube length; (LC4) thermal: 250 C sustained skin temperature, 40 C internal initial; (LC5) launch tube seal: 200 kPa internal over-pressure from motor start before nozzle clearance; (LC6) fin-lug point load: 500 N lateral at each fin lug during maneuver. Pass/fail: (R1) max principal strain in ply below 0.75% (conservative fracture limit for thermally-degraded CFRP at 250 C); (R2) max through-thickness shear below 40 MPa; (R3) launcher tube clearance (OD + thermal expansion + ovalization) below 104.5 mm inside 105 mm launcher; (R4) first ovalization mode >= 200 Hz; (R5) tube mass <= 1.2 kg per meter; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC3 external pressure >= 2.5. Deliverables: STEP + layup table, coupled thermal-structural FEA per case, modal, buckling, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a 1 m cylindrical airframe tube section of a shoulder-launched anti-UAV missile (MANPADS-class interceptor), with filament-wound CFRP [+/-45/0/90]s layup. The tube reaches 250 C aerodynamic skin temperature over a 6 s powered fly-out at Mach 2. No single governing airworthiness document is cited in the source; design to a conservative 0.75% principal-ply-strain allowable for thermally-degraded CFRP and a 40 MPa through-thickness shear allowable.

Envelope: 100 mm OD x 3 mm wall x 1 m length, launcher ID 105 mm (so OD + thermal + ovalization must remain < 104.5 mm), missile launch mass <= 15 kg, four fin-mount hardpoints.

Load cases.
- LC1 burnout: 40 g axial on 15 kg = 5886 N axial along the tube.
- LC2 terminal maneuver: 20 g lateral on 10 kg post-burn mass = 1962 N at the CG 0.6 m aft of nose.
- LC3 aerodynamic q: external q = 250 kPa at Mach 2 sea level, ~79 kN distributed over 1 m.
- LC4 thermal: 250 C skin, 40 C initial interior, sustained.
- LC5 motor-start seal: 200 kPa internal over-pressure before nozzle clearance.
- LC6 fin-lug point loads: 500 N lateral at each of four fin lugs during maneuver.

prompt.title: Shoulder-launched anti-UAV airframe tube
prompt.brief: CFRP filament-wound 100 mm OD airframe, Mach 2 terminal, 40 g burnout.
